jobs.title: Full-Stack Javascript Engineer II - Listen Team 3D Full Time Remote Cision
jobs.description: Post a Job Home Jobs Companies Pricing Post a Job Full-Stack Javascript Engineer II - Listen Team at Cision Full Time Remote Apply Now At Cision, we believe in empowering every individual to make an impact. Here, your voice is heard, your ideas are valued, and your unique perspective fuels our collective success. As part of our global team, you'll thrive in an environment that champions curiosity, collaboration, and innovation, all while making meaningful contributions to the brands we accelerate. Join us in shaping the future of communication and building authentic connections that matter. Whether you're solving complex problems or driving bold innovations, your growth is our success, and together, we’ll create the conversations of tomorrow. Empower your impact at Cision. Be seen, be understood, be you. We’re looking for a skilled JavaScript/TypeScript Engineer to join Brandwatch’s Listen team . You’ll help build and evolve a data-rich, customizable web application embedded within our partners’ platforms, enabling “light social listening” capabilities. This product allows partners to understand, explore, and engage with their customers across social media through actionable digital consumer insights. You’ll work within a cross-functional, autonomous team, collaborating closely with product managers, designers, and backend engineers to deliver high-quality features end-to-end. What You’ll Do Build and maintain scalable frontend features using React and TypeScript Develop responsive, high-quality UI components following modern React best practices Manage application state and async data flows using Redux Toolkit and React Query Integrate with backend APIs, handling loading, error, and authentication states effectively Monitor and improve frontend performance Troubleshoot, debug, and resolve production issues Write and maintain automated tests using Vitest and React Testing Library Participate in code reviews and contribute to engineering standards and best practices

jobs.title: Principal Frontend Developer - React 3D Full Time Capco
jobs.description: Post a Job Home Jobs Companies Pricing Post a Job Principal Frontend Developer - React at Capco Full Time Apply Now Principal Frontend Developer (React) Location: Glasgow (Hybrid) | Practice Area: Technology & Engineering | Type: Permanent Shape seamless digital experiences that define the future of financial services The Role As a Principal Frontend Developer at Capco, you’ll lead the development of scalable and secure web applications for global financial clients using modern JavaScript frameworks, primarily React. You’ll architect responsive, accessible, and elegant user interfaces and work closely with cross-functional teams to deliver transformative digital experiences. As a senior technical leader, you’ll mentor others, influence frontend strategy, and drive best practices. This role will sit in our Scotland office, with a need to potentially work in either Glasgow or Edinburgh depending on client expectations. What You’ll Do Architect and implement complex frontend systems using React and modern frameworks Apply UX/UI best practices to deliver pixel-perfect, accessible, and responsive applications Establish and champion frontend engineering standards across delivery teams Coach junior developers, promote knowledge sharing, and contribute to hiring efforts Collaborate with stakeholders to translate product needs into technical outcomes What We’re Looking For Strong JavaScript/TypeScript and React expertise; experience with Next.js, Vue.js, or Angular is a plus Experience with modern CSS techniques (Flexbox, Grid) and frameworks like SASS, Material UI, Bootstrap Familiarity with OAuth2, JWT, OpenID Connect, and secure frontend design principles Experience with Redux, Webpack, and modern testing frameworks like Jest, Mocha, or Enzyme Track record of delivering applications via CI/CD pipelines and containerised environments (Docker/Kubernetes) Bonus Points For Experience mentoring and coaching frontend developers Knowledge of cloud platforms and deployment strate

jobs.title: Z UI/UX Designer at ZILO
skills: 3+ years of experience in UX/UI design, ideally within fintech, SaaS, or complex product environments.
Proven experience managing or contributing to a design system, including component libraries and documentation.
Strong portfolio demonstrating user-centred design and problem solving.
Proficiency in design and prototyping in Figma, with experience working in shared design libraries.
Experience conducting user research, usability testing, and data-informed design decisions.
Strong understanding of interaction design, visual design, and accessibility standards.
Ability to work collaboratively with cross-functional teams including Product and Engineering.
Excellent communication and presentation skills.
Attention to detail with a passion for improving product usability and experience.
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, collaborative environment with evolving product needs.
jobs.description: Join the Future of Technology with ZILO™ At ZILO™, we're redefining what’s possible in technology. ZILO™ is the UK-based FinTech specialising in global asset and wealth management software, designed to scale and transform businesses of all types using our own developed AI Technology. Our mission is to digitalise the future of the global asset management industry. We are a team of experts with decades of combined experience at leading firms globally, who thrive in fast-paced environments and want to shape the future of technology. Every individual plays a key role in driving progress and making a real impact. We continuously strive to innovate and improve. Why work with us? At ZILO™, you'll be part of a dynamic and inclusive environment where creativity thrives. We offer the opportunity to work on cutting-edge technology, collaborate with talented individuals, and contribute to projects that have a real-world impact. We value continuous learning, personal growth, and providing our team with the resources they need to succeed. Ready to shape the future? Let’s talk. About the role As a UI/UX Designer at ZILO, you will help shape intuitive, high-quality product experiences for our platform. You will play a key role in evolving and maintaining the ZILO design system, ensuring consistency, accessibility, and scalability across our products. Working closely with Product Owners and the rest of the Design Team you will translate user needs and product goals into clear, thoughtful design solutions that improve usability and drive product value. There is an expectation you will attend our Churchill Place, Canary Wharf office, up to 3 days each week. Key Responsibilities Contribute to the evolution and governance of the ZILO design system, managing reusable components that are scalable, accessible, and aligned with our brand. Design intuitive user experiences that simplify complex workflows for our users. Collaborate with Product Managers and Engineers to ensure design solution

jobs.title: F UX/UI Designer at Fuku
jobs.description: About the company: Our client is a business consulting company in the financial technology sector, dedicated to transforming the way individuals and businesses manage their finances. Job responsibilities: - Design intuitive and engaging user interfaces for web and mobile applications. - Collaborate with product managers and developers to create user-centered designs. - Conduct user research and usability testing to gather insights and improve designs. - Create wireframes, prototypes, and high-fidelity mockups to communicate design ideas. - Ensure consistency in design across all platforms and devices. - Stay updated with the latest design trends and technologies to enhance user experience. - Work closely with the development team to ensure designs are implemented accurately. - Participate in brainstorming sessions and contribute creative ideas for product development. Job requirements: - Bachelor's degree in Design, Human-Computer Interaction, or related field. - Proven experience as a UX/UI Designer with a strong portfolio of design projects. - Proficiency in design tools such as Sketch, Adobe XD, Figma, or similar. - Strong understanding of user-centered design principles and best practices. - Excellent communication skills and ability to work collaboratively in a team environment. - Experience with responsive design and mobile-first design approaches. - Ability to manage multiple projects and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.

jobs.title: P Product Designer at Pavago
jobs.description: **Position:** Product Designer - UX/UI & Design Systems **Type:** Full-Time Remote | Working Hours: U.S. Client Business Hours **Location:** Brazil, Colombia, Argentina, Mexico **About This Opportunity:** Join a growing company as their Product Designer and shape the end-to-end experience of digital products. jobs.description: Build it, Ship it, Own it At Sangoma, we build the technology that keeps businesses connected. Every call, every transfer, every interaction matters. This role sits at the center of that experience.  You’ll be shaping how real work gets done, on devices used every day in high-stakes environments. From front desks to contact centers, your work will define how quickly, clearly, and reliably people communicate. If you care about clarity, speed, and systems that just work, this is your space. We are looking for an experienced UX Product Designer to play a critical role in shaping the user experience of Sangoma’s desk phones and telephony devices, focused on embedded, hardware-contained device experiences. Sangoma is dedicated to empowering organizations through reliable Unified Communications, UCaaS, CCaaS, VoIP, and Hybrid solutions. In this role, you will focus on how our desk phone products work, not just how they look—designing clear, efficient, and predictable iteraction models for task-critical communication workflows. While this is a remote position, we are looking for someone in either eastern or central time zones. Requirements Your Role: Design how communication actually happens. Own the end-to-end UX of Sangoma’s desk phone and device experiences, from interaction models to execution Design for embedded, hardware-constrained environments with physical inputs, limited screens, and real-time expectations Define task-driven workflows for core telephony functions: calling, transfers, conferencing, voicemail, call handling, and more Create clear, scalable interaction patterns that prioritize speed, predictability, and low cognitive load Translate complex communication workflows into simple, intuitive user experiences Develop UX artifacts including flows, wireframes, interaction specs, and system behaviors Partner closely with firmware engineers to ensure designs are implemented accurately and perform reliably in production Work with Product to align UX decisions w

jobs.title: Fat Batter Ice Cream is hiring Creative Assistant - Paid Internship
jobs.description: We’re Hiring: Creative Assistant (Part-Time, In-Person – Denver) Fat Batter Ice Cream is looking for a Creative Assistant to join our team in Denver. This is a 20 hrs/week, in-person role for someone who loves blending creativity with execution. You’ll work across design systems, digital operations, and social media , helping bring ideas to life and keep things moving. What You’ll Do Write and maintain design system documentation (clear, structured, useful) Assist with website updates (product listings, flavor drops, content updates) Manage and post on social media (Instagram, TikTok, etc.) Help create content for social (captions, simple visuals, coordinating posts) Organize and manage digital assets and product content Collaborate on creative ideas, campaigns, and storytelling Support day-to-day tasks that keep the brand sharp and consistent What We’re Looking For Strong writing skills (clear, concise, structured) Comfortable with tools like Figma, CMS platforms (Wix preferred), Google Docs Experience managing or contributing to social media accounts Detail-oriented—you catch what others miss Able to switch between creative thinking and execution Interest in design systems, branding, or digital products Bonus: experience with ecommerce or content strategy Why This Role is Different You’ll work directly with the founder You’ll touch product + brand + experience + social You won’t be stuck doing one thing—this role evolves You’ll help shape a growing brand in a meaningful way Details Location: In-person (Denver – South Broadway area) Hours: ~20 hours/week Type: Part-time (with potential to grow) How to Apply Send: A short intro about yourself Examples of UX, design, or social media work Anything that shows how you think

jobs.description: Company Description Hi! We're UW. We’re on a mission to take the headache out of utilities by providing them all in one place. One bill for energy, broadband, mobile and insurance and a whole lot of savings! We’re aiming to double in size as we help more people to stop wasting time and money—big ambitions, to be delivered by people like you. Got your attention? Read on… We work together. Your team and the people you will work with… This role spans multiple product teams and offers a balanced mix of strategic thinking and hands-on design. You’ll contribute across a range of initiatives focused on improving how customers interact with and understand the product, from key communications to core transactional experiences. You’ll work on important moments in the customer journey that shape clarity, confidence, and trust, helping ensure experiences feel clear, intuitive, and supportive. The role suits someone comfortable working across different problem spaces, curious about complex systems, and attentive to how individual touchpoints connect into a cohesive whole. You’ll be closely embedded within product teams, building strong relationships with product, engineering, and partner stakeholders. Success in this role comes from thoughtful collaboration, the ability to balance multiple perspectives, and a commitment to creating consistent, human-centred experiences across the product. Job Description We deliver progress. What you’ll do and how you will make an impact. As a UX Designer at UW you take ownership of design delivery within your product area. You work closely with your squad to create meaningful and cohesive user experiences, growing your confidence in managing end-to-end design work from discovery and concepting to delivery and iteration. You work collaboratively with product and engineering partners, balancing user needs with business goals. You seek and give feedback regularly, contributing to maintaining quality across the team. jobs.title: Z Principal UX Designer at Zearn
jobs.description: The Purpose of Zearn Zearn is the nonprofit educational organization whose mission is to inspire all kids to love learning math. Everything we do is driven by our belief that every kid is a math kid. Our top-rated learning platform is designed to catch kids up and move them forward in grade-level math. And it works. Large-scale efficacy research shows students who use Zearn make significant gains in math proficiency, including the students who start multiple grade levels behind. As a nonprofit, all of our work is driven by what is best for kids. At the same time, we operate as an intense and fast-moving technology company, inclusively solving hard problems that will impact current and future generations. The problems we solve require expertise in teaching, learning science, software development, design, video production, and countless other disciplines. As an organization that is always stretching to get to the next level of impact, every single person owns mission critical work. If you’re ready to join our Team of problem solvers, and own work that will drive us to achieve impact at scale, we are looking for you. Learn more about us at https://about.zearn.org/ . The purpose of the Principal UX Designer at Zearn is to lead the strategic design for our Educator app onboarding experience, directly impacting how thousands of teachers successfully adopt Zearn to help all students learn and love math. Through expert end-to-end design leadership, deep research expertise, and an ability to simplify complex systems, this role transforms ambiguous challenges into clear, intuitive experiences that meet educators where they are in their journey. This is achieved by: Owning the end-to-end design strategy for high-leverage educator onboarding and engagement experiences. Deliver complete, high-impact UX projects from initial research and strategy through final implementation, with a particular focus on reimagining the educator onboarding experience to drive understanding, adoptio

jobs.title: UI Designer - Anime ARPG Saguni Studio 2000$ - 3300$ Full-Time Details
jobs.description: To help us process your application efficiently, please start your email subject line with [UID]. We are looking for a UI/UX Designer to join our team on a stylized anime action RPG / gacha title for PC and mobile, including controller support. Our game is story-driven with a strong combat focus, a third-person camera, and a structure built around a main lobby hub where players manage their inventory, team, and individual characters before entering level-based missions of roughly 15 minutes each. Our visual and product direction is inspired by titles such as Digimon, Wuthering Waves, Arknights: Endfield, Zenless Zone Zero and Solo Leveling: Arise. We are looking for someone who can take strong ownership over the full UI process, from early UX thinking and wireframes to polished mockups, motion concepts, and high-quality final interface assets. Responsibilities • Own the UI/UX design process across the game in close collaboration with design, programming, and art direction. • Translate gameplay requirements, feature briefs, references, and visual goals into sketches, wireframes, user flows, mockups, and final UI designs. • Help shape the UX of a cross-platform experience for PC, mobile, and console. • Create UI that is not only visually appealing, but also highly readable, intuitive, and satisfying to use. • Translate wireframes into polished interface concepts with strong motion design thinking, helping avoid static or lifeless UI presentation. • Prepare clear handoff material and documentation so engineers can implement the UI efficiently. • Contribute to a growing UI guideline / style guide document and support process documentation in parallel with production. • Collaborate where needed with monetization-related design to create strong reward presentation and other high-impact interface moments. Must-haves • Professional experience as a UI Designer, UI/UX Designer, or similar role in the games industry. • Strong understanding of readability, information hierarchy

jobs.title: Technical Artist (Unity 3D - Claymation Focus) Edge of the World Studios Part-Time Remote, working around GMT + 3 Unity URP 3D Shader graph VFX Lighting technical art Details
jobs.description: Unity URP 3D Shader graph VFX Lighting technical art We’re hiring and looking to connect with: Technical Artist (Unity - Stylized / Claymation Focus) Edge of the World Studios is looking to connect with a Technical Artist to help shape the visual and technical foundation of Halima , a 2.5D narrative puzzle-platforming adventure currently in development. This role sits between art and engineering. You’ll work closely with our Lead Artist and Technical Lead to ensure that our visual direction translates effectively into Unity - whether that means building shader systems, refining lighting setups, developing artist-friendly tools, or troubleshooting performance and rendering challenges. The Project: Halima is a 2.5D narrative puzzle-platformer inspired by folklore, mythology, and surreal dreamscapes. Visually, the project draws from handcrafted, claymation-inspired aesthetics - tactile surfaces, soft lighting, subtle imperfections, and atmospheric depth. This role will be central to ensuring that our clay-inspired art direction translates cleanly, beautifully, and efficiently into Unity. The game draws inspiration from claymation and stop-motion film , aiming to achieve a handcrafted, tactile feel. We’re exploring how best to bring that sensibility into real-time rendering - and we’re looking for someone who can help us experiment, prototype, and refine that visual direction inside the engine. What you’ll be working on We’re looking to connect with Technical Artists who can: Develop and refine custom shaders that support a claymation-inspired surface language (subtle surface noise, animated texture variation, non-photorealistic shading). Further develop and troubleshoot base shaders to support features such as animated surface variation (e.g., randomized clay texture movement systems). Build simplified systems that allow artists to control materials, shader parameters, and visual variation without requiring engineering support. Design and implement lighting setups in U

jobs.title: Frontend Engineer (Web) Entity Full-Time Remote  (+/- 3 hours GMT) TypeScript Front-End Web Frameworks Details
jobs.description: TypeScript Front-End Web Frameworks About Entity Entity is on a mission to bring console-quality games to players everywhere - instantly, on any device. Just as software, music and film moved from download and own to click and stream, we believe gaming is shifting from Download and Install to Click and Play Now. Powered by the latest web technologies and built on the open web, Entity is creating a new home for next-gen instant games. For players, a destination to discover and enjoy incredible new titles (think Steam or YouTube for games). For developers, the tools, publishing power, and access to a truly global audience across every connected device. Founded by industry veterans and entrepreneurs, and backed by leading investors, we're assembling our core team. Joining Entity at this stage is a rare opportunity to help shape the future of gaming. About the role We're seeking a Web / Frontend Engineer with a strong design sensibility to help craft the core user experience of our web-based games platform. This role is primarily frontend-focused (~70%), centered on building beautiful, intuitive, and highly polished web interfaces. You'll be shaping how players browse, discover, launch, and engage with games - creating a rich, immersive Games Hub that feels fast, modern, and delightful to use. The remaining ~30% of the role involves collaborating on architecture, performance, and real-time features to ensure those interfaces scale smoothly and feel instant, even under heavy load. We're especially excited about candidates who care deeply about UX/UI details, interaction quality, layout, motion, and visual coherence - and who enjoy working closely with designers to turn strong concepts into pixel-perfect reality. Key Responsibilities Frontend & UX (Primary Focus) Design and build high-quality, visually polished user interfaces for a web-based games platform. Translate design concepts, wireframes, and prototypes into clean, maintainable, production-ready UI code. Focus on
